title: Disable holiday & vaction days in the workload widget (with custom capacity)
excerpt: We would like to add vacation and public vacations, without using the work schedule, in the calendar of the Workload widget. This would allow us to continue working based on set capacity values, but not have to sacrifice…

posts.author: NicoMutert (Nico Mutert)
posts.cookedHtml: We would like to add vacation and public vacations, without using the work schedule, in the calendar of the Workload widget. This would allow us to continue working based on set capacity values, but not have to sacrifice the ability to turn off unavailable days.

posts.author: Michaleiba (Michal Leiba)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I am Michal from the spot-nik team. Actually we just launched a new app- SuperPlanner and it does exactly that! I believe it answers your needs.

Try it now for free-

https://auth.monday.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=a72397016954aa33e272936988566142&response_type=install

Let me know what you think

posts.author: NicoMutert (Nico Mutert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Michal,

thank you, looks cool - I’m currently checking it out on your website (Hint: It would be good not to just send the install link when commenting, but also the info link to your website so one can inform about the product before installing).

One question that came up: Is SuperPlanner only working based on the “Timeline” column or can it also work with the “date” column, as we are not using timeline, but date.

Kind regards!

Nico

posts.author: SariHoltz (Sari Holtz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Nico, it’s Sari from the spot-nik team. At this time, SuperPlanner is only based on the Timeline column, but in the future it will be connected to dates as well. Are you using both Start Date and End Date columns instead of a timeline? I’d love to hear more about your setup so that it can help inform this feature development.

Thanks!

posts.author: NicoMutert (Nico Mutert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Sari,
we just use a single “Date” as a “Deadline Date” for our Content Creation Team.

So if we create a new, let’s say Graphic Design To Do, we set a deadline via the date column for this specific task, to define on which date the first version of this graphic has to be send in to the Project Manager for the first feedback loop.

We neither use start+end date, nor the timeline.

posts.author: SariHoltz (Sari Holtz)
posts.cookedHtml: @NicoMutert thanks for this information, I will pass it to our Dev team, and we’ll try to help with this use case.

posts.author: sophiasmith (Sophia Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: The Workload widget only treats days as unavailable when they come from the work schedule, so custom capacity alone does not handle public holidays or PTO. A practical workaround is to keep a work schedule for availability vacation and still use custom capacity for the actual hours so the load math stays accurate.

posts.author: NicoMutert (Nico Mutert)
posts.cookedHtml: Right now we created a “1 person Team” for every worker, so that every worker’s vacation can be edited separately. Public holidays needs to be entered manually though.

posts.author: SariHoltz (Sari Holtz)
posts.cookedHtml:
@NicoMutert

This can also be done in the SuperPlanner app

You can learn more about the app here - or schedule a demo if you’d like all your questions answered in real time.
